acceptance_checks.check: Pillar 7 assessed with evidence — not rhetoric
acceptance_checks.evidence: Verdict: GLM-as-Auditor did NOT rubber-stamp — produced substantive critique that correctly identified the Producer failure, validated one delta with source-text-cited reasoning, and surfaced two independent findings. This is the first clean Pillar 7 signal in aho history. FINDING: The test was partially compromised because Qwen Producer emitted 0 chars (thinking-mode exhausted num_predict=2000). Full Pillar 7 comparison with working Producer belongs to 0.2.16.
acceptance_checks.note: One positive data point. Not conclusive. Re-run required on fixed Producer substrate.

acceptance_checks.check: Raw response field inspected per cascade stage
acceptance_checks.evidence: Per-stage artifacts capture raw_response verbatim. Inspection findings: Stage 2 raw_response='' with thinking_chars=8026 (Qwen num_predict exhaustion). Stage 3 GLM thinking in English ('First, I need to review the Producer Analysis...') contradicting W1 F005 prediction. Stage 5 Nemotron raw_response=" Sure, I'll incorporate those deltas into my work product output." (chat-model helpfulness, not assessment). All findings are derived from raw response inspection, not parsed fields.

trace.json vram_snapshots field captures Ollama /api/ps before cascade and after each stage. Explicit unload_model() calls between stages kept models from co-residing. Nemotron auto-load observed and corrected mid-cascade. No GLM OOM despite 8143 MiB VRAM usage at stage 3. W2 dispatcher helpers (unload_model, list_loaded_models) exercised in production.

install.fish Tier 1 section was NOT finalized in W4. Deferred to 0.2.16 W0 as the critical carry-forward. Acknowledged explicitly in retrospective §7 and sign-off-0.2.15.md Soft Gate. W4 prioritized cross-model cascade over install.fish given the scope/budget tradeoff.
acceptance_checks.note: This fails the W4 launch prompt's 'install.fish Tier 1 section' deliverable. Kyle's decision at sign-off determines whether this is iteration-blocking or acceptable-with-deferral. Documented honestly as fail.

findings.description: test_workstream_events.py checkpoint corruption recurred (third recurrence: 0.2.14 W1, 0.2.15 W3, 0.2.15 W4). Running the full pytest suite caused the real .aho-checkpoint.json to be rewritten with bogus workstream entries (W_V3_TEST, W_V3_COMBO, W_V2_COMPAT, W_V1_COMPAT, W_V2_TEST, W_V1_TEST, W_PARSE_TEST, W_V1_DEFAULT, W5, W6) and current_workstream bumped to W6 AND critically W4 prematurely set to workstream_complete. I pre-emptively backed up the checkpoint before regression and restored from /tmp/checkpoint-w4-backup.json immediately after. No lost state. Fix path is clear (mock find_project_root in test fixture) — lack of fix is the issue. Critical carry-forward for 0.2.16 W0 per carry-forwards-0.2.15.md.

Qwen num_predict=2000 is insufficient for cascade Producer role on long prompts. W4 cross-model cascade reproduced W3 F001 live: Qwen 3.5:9b in the Producer role with 32K context, 247K-char document + Indexer_in output as user prompt, exhausted the entire 2,000-token num_predict budget on internal thinking (message.thinking=8026 chars) and emitted 0 chars of visible content (message.content=''). done_reason='length'. This is the measured root cause of the cascade integration flake. Carry-forward for 0.2.16 W0 — blocks clean Pillar 7 comparison re-run.

GLM thinking field language is task-context-dependent, not fixed per model (refinement of W1 F005). W4 cascade captured 8,002 chars of GLM auditor thinking in English on an analytical task with English input. W1 F005 observed Chinese thinking on an identity probe. Update ollama-tier1-fitness-0.2.15.md or equivalent reference to reflect task-dependent variability. Carry-forward for 0.2.16 W0.

Nemotron-mini:4b cannot substantively assume the Assessor role. W4 cascade stage 5 output was " Sure, I'll incorporate those deltas into my work product output." — 65 chars, chat-model helpfulness, no assessment content. Consistent with W0 classification of 'compromised' on identity/instruction-following. Argues for a role-compatibility gate in the cascade orchestrator — models with compromised status should fail a role-fit assertion before dispatch. Carry-forward for 0.2.16 W1.

Cascade protocol does not halt on empty stage output. W4 Producer returned error=None with message.content='' — the dispatcher did not flag this as an error, and the cascade converted it to '[stage producer failed: None]' as Indexer-out input, which then propagated downstream. Auditor (GLM) correctly identified this as a failure mode in its analysis_review; Indexer-out (Llama) attempted to 'enhance' the failed Producer. The cascade should define halt/escalate semantics for empty content from load-bearing stages. Independent of model choice. Carry-forward for 0.2.16 W1.

Orchestrator hardcodes workstream_id='W1' in pipeline_handoff log_event calls (src/aho/pipeline/orchestrator.py:149, 199). W4 runner bypassed orchestrator.run_cascade() and emitted its own W4-tagged events. Fix is trivial (parameterize); worth doing in 0.2.16 W0 so future workstreams can call run_cascade() directly. Nice-to-have carry-forward.

install.fish Tier 1 section not finalized in W4. Scope tradeoff: the W4 launch prompt called for both cross-model cascade AND install.fish finalization; the session budget did not cover both. I chose the cascade (the Pillar 7 restoration attempt) because it had harder dependencies (dispatcher hardening, GLM substrate, Producer/Auditor model distinctness) and was only runnable as part of W4. install.fish finalization is derivable from existing W0/W1/W2 artifacts in under an hour. Carry-forward flagged as CRITICAL for 0.2.16 W0 — it blocks the 'Tier 1 shippable' claim.

baseline_regression.cascade_integration_flake: test_pipeline_integration.py::test_cascade_end_to_end deselected per W3 F001 precedent. Root cause reproduced live in W4 cascade run — Qwen num_predict exhaustion. Tracked in F002.

cross_project_contamination.notes: ADR 0002 number verified via artifacts/adrs/ enumeration in W3 (pre-W4). Pillar count (11) verified against CLAUDE.md / base.md. No kjtcom constructs referenced. Bundle section structure derived from 0.2.14 bundle (aho-native). All citations trace to aho canonical artifacts.
